rationale: The SSOT cycle_model describes a deterministic single-cycle command acceptance with a 2-stage accept-then-update pipeline and combinational flag update. There is no multi-cycle latency, no pipelining with stalls, no backpressure, no out-of-order completion, and no variable-latency operations. The cycle behavior is fully captured by FunctionalModel.apply() which models the state transition atomically per transaction. A separate executable cycle-level (CL) model would be redundant with the functional model because: (1) command acceptance is always 0-cycle (same edge), (2) state update is always exactly 1-cycle latency, (3) flags are combinational (0-cycle after count), (4) no backpressure exists (cmd_ready=1 always), (5) throughput is exactly 1 command per cycle when cmd_valid is high. The cycle_model section is descriptive documentation only — it does not define any behavior that FunctionalModel.apply does not already model deterministically.
